description_long: This is a small, single-propeller military training airplane with a classic low-wing configuration and a tricycle landing gear. The fuselage is predominantly white with bold red-orange accents on the nose, tail, and wing tips, and it features prominent U.S. Navy insignia and identification numbers. The cockpit is enclosed with a clear canopy, positioned forward on the fuselage, and appears to be designed for one or two occupants. The tail section has a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer adorned with red stripes and additional markings. The wings are straight and low-mounted, each displaying military insignia and numbers. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the landing gear consists of two main wheels and a nose wheel, all fixed and visible.
description: A small, single-propeller U.S. Navy training airplane with a low-wing, tricycle landing gear, and bold red and white markings. Features an enclosed forward cockpit and conventional tail.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile, U.S. Navy insignia, red nose, and tail markings, with the propeller and landing gear clearly visible.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the straight wings, tail section, and the symmetry of the landing gear and markings on the wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other side's insignia, numbers, and the same red and white color scheme, with the propeller and landing gear visible.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the propeller, nose, forward cockpit canopy, and the symmetry of the wings and landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel, all fixed.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a clear canopy, likely single or tandem seating.
